mc.plugins

Interface IProcessFunction

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    AbstractionFunction, HideFunction, NFAtoDFAFunction, PruneFunction, SafeFunction, SimpFunction

    public interface IProcessFunction
    This interface describes a "function" in the LTS language variant in the use The function has the syntax of: @code{functionName{flag(s)}(Automaton(s))}

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      Automaton compose(java.lang.String id, java.lang.String[] flags, com.microsoft.z3.Context context, Automaton... automata)
      Execute the function on automata
      java.lang.String getFunctionName()
      Gets the method name when it is called (e.g.
      int getNumberArguments()
      Gets the number of automata to parse into the function
      java.util.Collection<java.lang.String> getValidFlags()
      Get the available flags for the function described by this interface (e.g.

    • Method Detail

      • getFunctionName

        java.lang.String getFunctionName()
        Gets the method name when it is called (e.g. abs in abs(A))
        Returns:
        the name of the function

      • getValidFlags

        java.util.Collection<java.lang.String> getValidFlags()
        Get the available flags for the function described by this interface (e.g. unfair in abs{unfair}(A)
        Returns:
        a collection of available flags (note, no variables may be flags)

      • getNumberArguments

        int getNumberArguments()
        Gets the number of automata to parse into the function
        Returns:
        the number of arguments

      • compose

        Automaton compose(java.lang.String id,
                  java.lang.String[] flags,
                  com.microsoft.z3.Context context,
                  Automaton... automata)
           throws CompilationException
        Execute the function on automata
        Parameters:
        id - the id of the resulting automaton
        flags - the flags given by the function (e.g. unfair in abs{unfair}(A)
        automata - a variable number of automata taken in by the function
        Returns:
        the resulting automaton of the operation
        Throws:
        CompilationException - when the function fails
